description: The American Mafia is a highly organized Italian-American criminal society that emerged in the late 19th century in impoverished Italian immigrant neighborhoods in New York, Chicago, New Orleans, and other US cities. It is structured into families and engages in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, extortion, drug trafficking, and murder. The organization cooperates with Italian organized crime groups such as the Sicilian Mafia, Camorra, and 'Ndrangheta. It remains active today with over 3,000 members and associates.

country_links.evidence_quote: Factions in Southern Ontario and Montreal in Canada
country_links.context: The American Mafia has factions in Southern Ontario and Montreal in Canada.
